title: Figma Make Launches Custom Skills — PRDs to Prototypes in One Command
why_it_matters: This fundamentally changes how designers and PMs collaborate on early product work. By encoding team conventions and connecting directly to tools like Notion and Confluence, Make Skills promise to eliminate the repetitive re-prompting that slows down AI-assisted prototyping. It also signals Figma's serious ambition to own the entire product development workflow — not just design.
whats_happening: On May 11, 2026, Figma shipped Custom Skills for Figma Make — a feature that lets users create reusable markdown-based instructions to encode their team's conventions and workflows directly into Make's AI prototyping system. Skills are called via slash commands in any Make prompt, meaning a team can run something like /build-from-prd paired with a Notion or Confluence connector to instantly turn a product requirements document into a standards-compliant interactive prototype. Each skill can also pull live context from tools a team already uses — fetching design systems from Notion or PRDs from Google Drive and applying both automatically. Currently skills are per-user, with org-wide sharing coming soon. The launch was part of Figma's broader May release push, which also included MCP tools for FigJam that let AI agents generate architecture diagrams and plan engineering work collaboratively alongside design.

title: Anthropic Nears $1 Trillion Valuation as Revenue Hits $30B — Claude Code Is the Engine
why_it_matters: The explosive growth of Claude Code — and the corporate customers like Uber and Netflix now depending on it — is rapidly reshaping what 'AI-assisted product development' means at scale. For designers and product teams, this signals that agentic coding tools are no longer experiments: they're becoming foundational infrastructure. Understanding how these tools change engineering workflows directly affects how designers collaborate with developers and define handoff.
whats_happening: This week, Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei revealed that the company achieved 80x annualized revenue growth in Q1 2026 — a figure he described as 'just crazy' and 'too hard to handle.' The company's annualized revenue has reached $30 billion, up from roughly $9 billion at the end of 2025, driven largely by Claude Code, its agentic coding tool whose weekly active users have doubled since January 1 and whose run-rate revenue alone now exceeds $2.5 billion. Enterprise customers including Uber and Netflix are among the wave of corporate adopters. The growth has been so swift that Anthropic's compute infrastructure has struggled to keep pace, forcing the company into a deal with SpaceX to access 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs and 300 megawatts of new capacity. On May 7, the Financial Times reported that Anthropic is now weighing a new $50 billion funding round at a near-$900 billion valuation — which would see it surpass rival OpenAI as the world's most valuable AI startup ahead of a potential IPO as early as October 2026.

title: OpenAI Opens ChatGPT Ads Manager to All US Businesses — Conversational UI Becomes Ad Real Estate
why_it_matters: For UX and product designers, this is a significant new constraint and opportunity: conversational interfaces now need to accommodate clearly labeled sponsored content without disrupting the integrity of the interaction. It also raises immediate questions about trust design — how do you preserve the feeling of an unbiased AI assistant when commercial interests are adjacent to every response? These are deeply UX-first problems that will define AI product quality in the months ahead.
whats_happening: On May 5, 2026, OpenAI opened a beta self-serve Ads Manager to all US advertisers, simultaneously removing its previous $50,000 minimum spend requirement and introducing cost-per-click bidding alongside the existing CPM model. Ads appear as clearly labeled, subtly tinted boxes at the bottom of AI responses for Free and Go tier users, with targeting based on the context of the user's conversation rather than keyword or behavioral profiling. The expansion came just days after a May 7 blog post announced the pilot would extend to the UK, Mexico, Brazil, Japan, and South Korea. OpenAI has committed to an 'answer independence' principle — ads cannot influence ChatGPT's responses — and advertisers receive only aggregated performance data, not access to individual conversations. Early performance data from Criteo showed AI-referred conversion rates approaching twice those of traditional search in several categories. OpenAI expects to generate $2.5 billion in ad revenue this year, with an eye toward $100 billion by 2030.

title: Apple's Liquid Glass Rolls Out Across iOS 26 — The Biggest OS Design Shift Since iOS 7
why_it_matters: Liquid Glass is not a cosmetic refresh — it fundamentally changes how designers must think about layering, depth, transparency, and background-context when designing for Apple platforms. Any team building iOS or macOS apps will need to audit and update custom UI components, since only system controls get the treatment automatically. More broadly, the move is already setting a new visual benchmark that is influencing web and cross-platform interface design far beyond the Apple ecosystem.
whats_happening: Apple's Liquid Glass — introduced at WWDC 2025 — is now rolling out across iOS 26, iPadOS 26, macOS Tahoe 26, watchOS 26, and tvOS 26, marking the company's most significant design overhaul since the flat design shift of iOS 7. The system is built around a dynamic translucent material that reflects and refracts surrounding content, responding to light, motion, scrolling speed, and user interaction. Interface controls, tab bars, navigation bars, toolbars, and app icons are all rebuilt using Liquid Glass, creating a sense of floating depth rather than flat layers. Apple's developer gallery already showcases early third-party adoption — apps like AllTrails, Fantastical, and GoodLinks have shipped redesigns that lean into the new material language. The shift requires designers to rethink layering and opacity strategies entirely; when teams recompile with Xcode 26, system controls update automatically, but any custom UI components that aren't updated will create a jarring visual inconsistency. Apple has signalled that retaining the old design will not be an option after iOS 27 ships next year.

title: Google I/O 2026 Countdown: Agentic Coding, Gemini 4, and Android XR Smart Glasses in Focus
why_it_matters: Google I/O sets the development agenda for one of the world's largest platforms. The explicit focus on agentic coding and multimodal AI this year signals that designers will increasingly need to collaborate with AI agents operating inside development pipelines — not just use AI as a design aid. The expected Android XR smart glasses previews are also directly relevant to product designers thinking about spatial and ambient interface paradigms beyond the screen.
whats_happening: Google I/O 2026 kicks off on May 19 at the Shoreline Amphitheater in Mountain View, with a main keynote at 10am PT followed by a developer keynote focused on 'agentic coding' at 1:30pm PT. The event is widely expected to be Google's most AI-dense showcase in years, with confirmed session topics including agentic coding tools, Gemini model updates, multimodal media generation, and robotics integration. Gemini Nano 4 — previewed in early April — is expected to be a centrepiece, with two variants promising 3x speed improvements over prior Nano models and positioning as the on-device AI backbone for Android 17. Hardware previews are also anticipated, including potential reveals related to Android XR — Google's operating system for AR devices currently available on Samsung's Galaxy XR headset, with smart glasses partnerships with Warby Parker and Gentle Monster still pending public updates. Developers are also watching for updates to Vertex AI, Google AI Studio, Firebase AI integration, and the Chrome AI layer that turns the browser into an ambient AI assistant.
